title: Admins can now transfer automation ownership while disabling previous owner!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
New feature alert

Account admins are now able to seamlessly transfer automation ownership to a new owner when disabling a user in the platform.

The pop-up will appear prior to disabling the user, ensuring that workflows continue to run uninterrupted as automations remain active while the ownership is transferred to a new owner.

Screen Shot 2023-06-20 at 11.13.29 am2840×1110 179 KB

Important to note

  • For security reasons, this feature is only available for automations, not integrations at this time.
  • Transferring ownership will happen on a user-by-user basis, and is not supported when bulk disabling users.

We hope this is a welcomed update! If it is or isn’t, we want to hear your thoughts in the comments below!

posts.author: kpatterson (Kathleen Patterson)
posts.cookedHtml: Could the transfer of board ownership be handled the same way?

posts.author: Johanna (Johanna Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Is there a best practice you can recommend? i.e. For employees leaving the company, should the automation ownership go to an admin account, or something else? What is your recommendation?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Ooh, I like your thinking! This is definitely a suggestion I will with share with the dedicated team. In the meantime, we encourage you to submit this as a feature request so other community member can add their vote to get this on our developers priority list

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Johanna! Whilst transparently, it does depend on each case, I would say that if you don’t have a specific user in place to take ownership, I’d definitely say transferring to an Admin is a smart option here, primarily so they are in control of the automations that are running across boards on an account level. Alternatively, you could also transfer ownership to a user that has access to the most boards that the previous (automation) owner had access too - simply due to that fact that they’re most familiar with the workflow and automation process in those specific boards. Hope this helps!

posts.author: kpatterson (Kathleen Patterson)
posts.cookedHtml: Done! (pending moderator approval anyway)

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think this is a great feature!

I do have one question: we’re a fairly large (and growing!) organization, and I don’t see a way to know whether a user we’re about to disable actually owns any automations? If they do, then we need to figure out who on their team (or in their workspace) we’d assign those automations to and then let that person know they need to re-enable them.

If they don’t have any automations then I don’t have to worry about any of that and I can just assign the non-existent automations to me and move on.

Put simply, when the user owns automations then we have additional administrative steps to follow - but we don’t have that visibility.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks so much for sharing your comment Brooks - this feedback is really helpful. I shared this directly with the product manager in charge who confirmed that …

we are currently evaluating multiple solutions to see, manage and transfer user’s automations and integrations across the account.

We will continue to share updates with you as soon as we can

posts.author: Louise22 (Louise Morel)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s been quite a while since the last post on this topic, but I thought I would drop in a comment. I’m an Administrator and yesterday I disabled people who were assisting us to set up Monday as we have completed our work with then. I actually created 90% of the Automations but when I disabled the Monday.com partners, I changed ownership to myself. I have had to go through every one of our jobs and re-activate the Majority of the Automations. It’s as if I didn’t create them so wasn’t the Owner. I’m also having issues with Webhooks. I am showing up as the Owner, but when I try to Enable the Automations, they won’t stay on. It’s so weird that some of the Automations still have the name of one of the people I have disabled as the owner. It would be great if I didn’t have to go through every job board and check every Automation is turned on when ownership is changed. Why do they have to turn off when the owner is changed? It’s time consuming and on top of that, some of the Automations (with Webhooks) won’t work.
